en: TO THE CHOIRMASTER: WITH STRINGED INSTRUMENTS. A MASKIL OF DAVID. Give ear to my prayer, O God, and hide not yourself from my plea for mercy!
zh: 神啊！求你傾聽我的禱告， 不要隱藏起來不聽我的懇求。

en: Attend to me, and answer me; I am restless in my complaint and I moan,
zh: 求你留心聽我，應允我； 我在苦惱中必不安寧，唉哼難過；

en: because of the noise of the enemy, because of the oppression of the wicked. For they drop trouble upon me, and in anger they bear a grudge against me.
zh: 這都是由於仇敵的聲音， 和惡人的欺壓； 因為他們使禍患臨到我的身上， 怒氣沖沖地迫害我。

en: My heart is in anguish within me; the terrors of death have fallen upon me.
zh: 我的心在我裡面絞痛， 死亡的恐怖落在我身上。

en: Fear and trembling come upon me, and horror overwhelms me.
zh: 懼怕和戰兢臨到我， 驚恐籠罩著我。

en: And I say, "Oh, that I had wings like a dove! I would fly away and be at rest;
zh: 我說：“但願我有鴿子一般的翅膀， 我就飛走，得以安居。

en: yes, I would wander far away; I would lodge in the wilderness; Selah
zh: 看哪！我必逃往遠處， 在曠野裡住宿。

en: I would hurry to find a shelter from the raging wind and tempest."
zh: 我要趕快到我避難的地方去， 逃避狂風暴雨。”

en: Destroy, O Lord, divide their tongues; for I see violence and strife in the city.
zh: 主啊！擾亂惡人的計謀， 使他們的意見 分歧， 因為我在城中看見了強暴和爭競的事。

en: Day and night they go around it on its walls, and iniquity and trouble are within it;
zh: 惡人日夜在城牆上繞行， 在城裡盡是邪惡與禍害；

en: ruin is in its midst; oppression and fraud do not depart from its marketplace.
zh: 城中也有毀滅人的事， 欺壓和詭詐不離城裡的街道。

en: For it is not an enemy who taunts me- then I could bear it; it is not an adversary who deals insolently with me- then I could hide from him.
zh: 原來不是仇敵辱罵我， 如果是仇敵，我還可以忍受； 也不是恨我的人向我狂妄自大， 如果是恨我的人，我還可以躲避他。

en: But it is you, a man, my equal, my companion, my familiar friend.
zh: 但你是和我同等地位的人， 是我的良友，我的知己。

en: We used to take sweet counsel together; within God's house we walked in the throng.
zh: 我們常在一起密談， 我們在 神的殿中與群眾同行。

en: Let death steal over them; let them go down to Sheol alive; for evil is in their dwelling place and in their heart.
zh: 願死亡忽然臨到他們身上， 願他們活活下到陰間去， 因為在他們中間，就是在他們的住所裡，盡是邪惡。

en: But I call to God, and the LORD will save me.
zh: 至於我，我卻要求告 神， 耶和華就必拯救我。

en: Evening and morning and at noon I utter my complaint and moan, and he hears my voice.
zh: 無論在晚上、早晨或中午， 我都哀訴唉哼； 他必聽我的聲音。

en: He redeems my soul in safety from the battle that I wage, for many are arrayed against me.
zh: 他救贖我的性命脫離攻擊我的人， 使我得著平安， 儘管攻擊我的人的確很多。

en: God will give ear and humble them, he who is enthroned from of old, Selah because they do not change and do not fear God.
zh: 神必聽見， 那從亙古坐著為王的必使他們受苦， 因他們不肯改變， 也不敬畏 神。

en: My companion stretched out his hand against his friends; he violated his covenant.
zh: 他違背了自己的約， 伸手攻擊那些與他和好的人。

en: His speech was smooth as butter, yet war was in his heart; his words were softer than oil, yet they were drawn swords.
zh: 他的口比奶油光滑， 他的心卻懷著爭戰的意圖； 他的話比油還柔和， 其實卻是拔了出來的刀。

en: Cast your burden on the LORD, and he will sustain you; he will never permit the righteous to be moved.
zh: 你要把你的重擔卸給耶和華， 他必扶持你； 他永遠不會讓義人動搖。

en: But you, O God, will cast them down into the pit of destruction; men of blood and treachery shall not live out half their days. But I will trust in you.
zh: 神啊！你必使惡人墮入滅亡的深坑裡； 流人血和行詭詐的人必活不到半世； 至於我，我必倚靠你。
